prose:
Method–provenance summary (frontier-orbital gap)
Property: HOMO–LUMO energy gap (Koopmans-type difference) = 6.6310857 eV
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP, **no density fitting** (exact four-center integrals)
Geometry provenance: user-supplied benzene geometry (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz); single-point only (no re-optimization)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
Total molecular charge 0; spin multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: PySCF  (invoked via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill)
SCF convergence: converged in 8 cycles; gradient/energy criteria satisfied
Warnings: no warnings reported by the engine
Integrity check: status ok, trustworthy = true (all automated checks passed)
Live log: calculation wrote a live SCF/analysis transcript to a frontier_<timestamp>.out file in the working directory (path echoed at run start by chemkit; available for inspection)

Result
• HOMO energy  −7.0468329 eV (index 20)
• LUMO energy  −0.41574728 eV (index 21)
→ HOMO–LUMO gap 6.6310857 eV (gas-phase B3LYP/def2-TZVP single-point on supplied benzene geometry)

This value is a purely computed quantity (`computed` provenance) obtained with the chemkit `frontier-orbitals` skill using the options:
  --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1
No density-fitting approximation was employed.
